top_signals.title: SoftBank Taps Retail Investors With $1.6 Billion Yen Bond Deal
top_signals.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to raise ¥260 billion ($1.6 billion) through another sale of subordinated bonds aimed mainly at individual investors, about two months after a similar retail offering.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: More Tankers Make It Through the Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Two liquefied natural gas carriers and a supertanker have traversed the Strait of Hormuz over the past couple of days, with the LNG vessels heading for Pakistan and China, and the tanker, loaded with Iraqi crude, is en route to China. According to a Reuters report, the vessels used the route that Iran has told all ships to use from now on. The LNG carriers are loaded with Qatari gas, the report said, citing data from LSEG and Kpler. At least one of the vessels was loaded in late March. The supertanker carrying Iraqi Basrah crude loaded its cargo…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: European stocks expected to open higher as U.S.-Iran talks continue
top_signals.summary: Stocks in Europe are tracking their Asian counterparts higher after Japan's Nikkei 225 breached 65,000 for the first time on Monday.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Italy’s Cassa Depositi Plans to Raise Nexi Stake Up to 29.9%
top_signals.summary: Italy’s state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti SpA plans to raise its stake in Nexi SpA to as much as 29.9%, backing the Italian digital payment company’s long-term strategy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Australia Set to Include Existing LNG Contracts in Reservation
top_signals.summary: Australia’s requirement for liquefied natural gas producers to reserve a fifth of exports for local use is set to apply to all projects and existing contracts, intensifying pressure on them to secure more supply.
